Door1 Relay Tests
info:
# Dry Test Setup
Set DUT to Dry Relay Mode
Turn Door1 & Door2 Relays OFF
Connect 5VDC DUT Voltage to DOOR1_COM & DOOR2_COM
# Door1 Dry Test
Check Door1 COM voltage = 2.15VDC
Check Door1 NC voltage = 2.15VDC
Check Door1 NO voltage = 0VDC
Turn Door1 Relay ON
Check Door1 NC voltage = 0VDC
Check Door1 NO voltage = 5VDC
Turn Door1 Relay OFF
# Door1 Wet 12V
Check Door1 COM voltage = 0VDC
Check Door1 NC voltage = 12VDC
Check Door1 NO voltage = 0VDC
Turn Door1 Relay ON
Check Door1 COM voltage = 0VDC
Check Door1 NC voltage = 0VDC
Check Door1 NO voltage = 12VDC
Turn Door1 Relay OFF
# Door1 Wet 24V
Check Door1 COM voltage = 0VDC
Check Door1 NC voltage = 24VDC
Check Door1 NO voltage = 0VDC
Turn Door1 Relay ON
Check Door1 COM voltage = 0VDC
Check Door1 NC voltage = 0VDC
Check Door1 NO voltage = 24VDC
Turn Door1 Relay OFF

name: Check Door1 Dry (OFF)
Check Door1 Dry (ON)
Check Door1 Wet 12V (OFF)
Check Door1 Wet 12V (ON)
Check Door1 Wet 24V (OFF)
Check Door1 Wet 24V (ON)

Door1 LED Check

Test Flow
info:
# Run Once
## Initalize Hardware
 1. Initialize Global Variables
 2. Initialize Connected Hardware
    -  LabJack T7
    -  Siglent Power Supply

# Run Each Test
## Prompt User for Serial Number & DUT Installed
1. Prompt user to scan Serial Number
2. Instruct user to insert DUT
    - Prompt will disapear once DUT is installed top is closed and OK is pressed

## Voltage Tests
1. Turn on Power Supply

name: Functional Test Start
info:
- This is the start of the functional test section
- This will be run for each DUT that is tested

name: Refresh Page
info: Need to do a browser refresh when so the Config and Admin forms load with data after node restart

name: PCB Boot Delay
info: Give the PCB time to boot and the voltages to level out.

name: function 8
Clear All Min/Max
I think I will only need to do this once at the start of the test.
Input Tests
info:
# Test Sequence
The plan was to check the processors A/D inputs
and then short and check the A/D again

There is an issue with the fixture where Door1 In2
does not want to switch so in the short term 
I'm just going to ensure that the A/D reading is
in the correct range

This should be good enough for now.


# Improvment

name: Reader Tests
info:
Measure Reader 12VDC
Configure fixture relays for Wiegand tests
Send Clear Wiegand Count Message
Trigger ESP32 to send Wiegand data
Clear Global Reader data before read
Send Read Bit Count Message
Check bit counts (1's and 0's)
Set Reader LED to HIGH
Verify Reader LED is HIGH
Set Reader LED to LOW
Verify Reader LED is LOW
Set Buzzer to HIGH on the LabJack
Verify Buzzer is HIGH
Set Buzzer to LOW on the LabJack
Verify Buzzr is LOW

name: function 11
Power DUT and Wait for Boot

Check D1 IN2
Check D2 IN1
Check D2 IN2
Decode JSON Respose

rules.to: {"title":"PCB Serial Number","content":"Please Scan or Enter Serial Number","okText":"OK","cancelText":"Cancel","textLabel":"Serial Number"}

name: Prompt User for Serial Number & DUT Install
info:
## Serial Number Prompt
- If S/N is entered and OK pressed add to global.serial_num
- If no S/N and OK pressed or Cancel pressed, then do not save any of the results

## Insert DUT Prompt
- Wait for DUT to be inserted and fixture top closed before OK button is available
- If Cancel is pressed, return to Serial Number Prompt
